inline_comments.message:
please use readinessCheck components
in values.yaml:
global:
  msbEnabled: true
readinessCheck:	
  sharedDatabase:
      name: shared-database
      containers:
        - '{{ include "common.release" . }}-{{ include "common.name" . }}-config-job'
  localDatabase:
      name: local-database
      containers:
        - '{{ include "common.mariadbService" .}}'
  msb:
    name: msb
    containers:
        - msb-iag

in requirements.yaml:
  - name: readinessCheck
    version: ~6.x-0
    repository: '@local'

here:
      initContainers: 
      {{- if global.mariadbGalera.localCluster }}
      {{ include "common.readinessCheck.waitFor" (dict "dot" . "wait_for" .Values.readinessCheck.wait_for.localDatabase ) | indent 6 | trim }}
      {{- else }}
      {{ include "common.readinessCheck.waitFor" (dict "dot" . "wait_for" .Values.readinessCheck.wait_for.sharedDatabase ) | indent 6 | trim }}
      {{- end }}
      {{- if .Values.global.msbEnabled }}
      {{ include "common.readinessCheck.waitFor" (dict "dot" . "wait_for" .Values.readinessCheck.wait_for.msb ) | indent 6 | trim }}
      {{- end }}

> missing a space here no?


There are six spaces here.According to our test, the make operation works normally. If we add another or more space, the make operation will report "did not find expected key" error.
So I think the writing here is correct
would be great if you could use here the readinessCheck template
this is provided internally by the template so you should remove it
why do you need this IP? Couln't you just listen on 0.0.0.0?
This modification requires the readnesscheck module to support job detection. What about your suggestion?
We need this IP to register our services to MSB
Is really MSB so broken that you register to it using Pod IP which may come and gone instead of service IP which is more pernament?
why do we need SERVICE_IP now?
I asked the same question. It's for registering in our favorite MSB
